title: Microsoft Graph Security - Alerts And Incidents APIs
applies_to: Microsoft Graph security alert triage and incident response workflows

confirmed_facts.fact: The current-generation security alerts are exposed from /security/alerts_v2 and incidents from /security/incidents.
Alerts and incidents pull alert data from security provider services that are part of or integrated with Microsoft 365 Defender, then correlate related alerts into incidents and include evidence.
The overview identifies rich alerts and incidents as the recommended replacement for the legacy security alerts API.
List incidents supports expanding alerts by using $expand=alerts.

The provider set for the newer alerts and incidents includes Microsoft Entra ID Protection, Microsoft 365 Defender, Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps, Microsoft Defender for Endpoint, Microsoft Defender for Identity, Microsoft Defender for Office 365, Microsoft Purview Data Loss Prevention, and Microsoft Purview Insider Risk Management.
The legacy /security/alerts API is deprecated and will be removed by April 2026.

implementation_notes: Use incidents as the default investigation object when the user wants the attack story or broader context, and use alerts_v2 when the user wants the underlying detections or alert queue.
When you need both the incident and the related alerts in one call, use incidents with $expand=alerts.
Prefer application permissions for automation when provider-specific user-role requirements make delegated access inconsistent, especially for Defender for Endpoint-backed data.
Avoid building new workflows on legacy alerts unless you are explicitly migrating or comparing the deprecated API.
caveats: Legacy alerts and modern alerts or incidents are different surfaces with different schemas and lifecycle expectations.
Provider-specific behavior can still vary behind the unified schema, including role requirements and patch or subscription support.
Some Microsoft Learn detail pages for these APIs may require authorization, so deeper field-by-field extraction should be done only when a project targets these resources directly.
